was a Christian [liberal arts](/wiki/Liberal_arts "Liberal arts") college in [Pasadena, California](/wiki/Pasadena,_California "Pasadena, California"), United States. It was located at 450 Avenue 64, Pasadena, California 91105.

Rhoads, Gladys Titzck, Nancy Titzck Anderso. [*McIntire: Defender of Faith and Freedom*](https://books.google.com/books?id=KYqKcJYPlMwC&pg=PA159). p. 159.`{{cite book}}`: CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_multiple_names:_authors_list "Category:CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list"))

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Highland College

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Type | Christian liberal arts |
| Established | May 4, 1950 (1950-05-04) |
| Affiliation | [Bible Presbyterian Church](/wiki/Bible_Presbyterian_Church "Bible Presbyterian Church") |
| Location | [Pasadena, California](/wiki/Pasadena,_California "Pasadena, California") , US |

| Map | |

**Highland College** was a Christian [liberal arts](/wiki/Liberal_arts "Liberal arts") college in [Pasadena, California](/wiki/Pasadena,_California "Pasadena, California"), United States. It was located at 450 Avenue 64, Pasadena, California 91105.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Highland_College&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Highland College was founded under the leadership of the Dr. [Clyde J. Kennedy](/wiki/Clyde_J._Kennedy "Clyde J. Kennedy") on May 4, 1950 as an agency of the [Bible Presbyterian Church](/wiki/Bible_Presbyterian_Church "Bible Presbyterian Church").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The Annandale Country Club property was purchased and used as the campus. Subsequently, [Robert G. Rayburn](/wiki/Robert_G._Rayburn "Robert G. Rayburn") became president.

On March 1, 1955, Rayburn was dismissed.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) Consequently, a split occurred and led to the establishment of [Covenant College](/wiki/Covenant_College "Covenant College").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Highland_College&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

* [Francis Schaeffer](/wiki/Francis_Schaeffer "Francis Schaeffer")[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Highland_College&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Leadership")]

* John E. Carson (1950-1952)
* [Robert G. Rayburn](/wiki/Robert_G._Rayburn "Robert G. Rayburn") (1952–1955)
* [Lynn Gray Gordon](/wiki/Lynn_Gray_Gordon "Lynn Gray Gordon") (1955–1957)
* [Robert E. Kofahl](/w/index.php?title=Robert_E._Kofahl&action=edit&redlink=1 "Robert E. Kofahl (page does not exist)") (1957–unknown)
